summary: Fix premature rethrow at OP_UWRETHROW so a nested unwind-protect inside an outer cleanup doesn't skip the code after it.

items.title: Defer rethrow at OP_UWRETHROW when restoring a saved pending state
items.description:
Bug: in src/core/vm.c the OP_UWRETHROW handler (around line 3462) currently restores the saved pending-throw snapshot into the cl_pending_* globals when cl_pending_throw == 0 at entry, then falls through to the rethrow logic. When this is an INNER unwind-protect running inside an OUTER cleanup that has an in-progress non-local transfer, the inner UWPROT arming pushed the active (pending_throw=1, tag, value, mv*, error) onto cl_saved_pending_stack and cleared the globals to 0. After the inner protected body and inner cleanup complete normally, the inner OP_UWRETHROW restores cl_pending_throw=1 from saved and then immediately performs the rethrow — longjmping straight to the catch/restart target and abandoning the rest of the OUTER cleanup body (the code that runs *after* the call site of the inner UWP). The fix is to distinguish 'pending was active when we entered UWRETHROW' from 'we just restored a parked pending from saved'. Only the former should rethrow at THIS UWRETHROW; the latter must leave the pending state parked in the globals so that the enclosing UWP's UWRETHROW (or an enclosing CATCH/BLOCK frame, if there is no enclosing UWP) picks it up after the enclosing cleanup body finishes.

Implementation:
  1. In src/core/vm.c, OP_UWRETHROW (currently src/core/vm.c:3462-3568):
     - At the very top of the handler, capture `int rethrow_active = (cl_pending_throw != 0);` BEFORE popping/restoring anything. This reflects whether a throw was already in flight when the cleanup body completed (either because this is the UWP that captured it and nothing in the cleanup cleared it, or because a NEW NLX was initiated inside the cleanup body itself).
     - Keep the existing pop of cl_saved_pending_stack into a local `saved` and the existing `if (cl_pending_throw == 0) { restore from saved ... }` block — that block remains the only path that mutates the globals from the saved snapshot.
     - Guard the two rethrow blocks (`if (cl_pending_throw == 1) { ... type-1 rethrow ... }` at src/core/vm.c:3496 and `else if (cl_pending_throw == 2) { ... type-2 rethrow ... }` at src/core/vm.c:3530) with the captured `rethrow_active` flag. If `!rethrow_active` skip both rethrow blocks and fall through to VM_BREAK so execution continues with the next opcode (which, per compile_unwind_protect in src/core/compiler_special.c:1241, is the VALUES-LIST call that restores the protected form's multiple values from the list_slot — and that slot WAS populated because the inner UWP took the normal path).
     - Do NOT change the existing OP_UWPROT longjmp branch — its update of saved_pending_stack[top-1] with the active throw is still required so the OUTER UWRETHROW sees the right saved entry should an inner UWP swallow/restore the pending state before reaching it.
     - Update the leading comment block of OP_UWRETHROW to explain the new rule: 'A UWRETHROW only re-initiates the pending NLX when one was active at entry (set by our own longjmp branch or by a new NLX during cleanup). If we just restored a saved pending from a nested UWP arming, leave it parked in the globals — the enclosing UWP's UWRETHROW (or the next NLX search point) will see it. Re-initiating it here would skip cleanup code in the enclosing UWP that runs after our call site.'

  2. Regression tests in tests/test_condition.c — add directly after lisp_nested_uwp_in_cleanup_does_not_truncate_transfer (around tests/test_condition.c:956) and register them in the RUN section near tests/test_condition.c:1195:
     - TEST(lisp_nested_uwp_in_cleanup_runs_code_after_nested_uwp): the exact repro from the bug — assert that the result is the list (:CLEANUP-START :INSIDE-NESTED-UWP :CLEANUP-AFTER-NESTED :HANDLER). Use a (let ((log nil)) ... (reverse log)) idiom so the test is hermetic and the order is explicit. Use `(flet ((nested-uwp (thunk) (unwind-protect (funcall thunk) nil)) (mk (x) (push x log) x)) (restart-case (unwind-protect (invoke-restart 'abort) (progn (mk :cleanup-start) (nested-uwp (lambda () (mk :inside-nested-uwp))) (mk :cleanup-after-nested))) (abort () (mk :handler))) (reverse log))` and ASSERT_STR_EQ to '(:CLEANUP-START :INSIDE-NESTED-UWP :CLEANUP-AFTER-NESTED :HANDLER)'.
     - TEST(lisp_two_deep_nested_uwp_in_cleanup_runs_code_after_both): two levels of nested UWP inside the outer cleanup; assert every :AFTER-* marker fires in order, the handler runs LAST, and the abort transfer still completes.
     - TEST(lisp_nested_uwp_in_cleanup_runs_code_after_for_error_propagation): use handler-case + error path so that cl_pending_throw == 2 (error) is the parked state instead of throw (==1); the cleanup code after the nested UWP must still run, and :caught must come back from handler-case. Mirrors lisp_error_in_body_nested_uwp_in_cleanup_propagates at tests/test_condition.c:986 but with code after the nested UWP that must execute.

  3. Mirror tests in tests/amiga/run-tests.lisp — add directly after the existing 'nested-uwp-in-cleanup does not truncate transfer' check at tests/amiga/run-tests.lisp:4966. Use the same (check NAME EXPECTED EXPR) style as adjacent tests. Cover: (a) the basic 'code after nested-uwp in cleanup runs' case returning the same 4-element keyword list as the host test, (b) the error-propagation variant.

  4. Run `make test` from /workspace; it must pass. Do NOT bump CL_FASL_VERSION — this change is purely VM runtime behavior; no FASL byte layout / bytecode encoding / opcode semantics change. (OP_UWPROT still pushes the same saved-pending slot, OP_UWPOP/OP_UWRETHROW emission is unchanged, no new opcodes.)
